features.summary: Apple reported a record revenue of $83.4 billion for Q4 FY2021, marking a 29% increase year-over-year, despite supply constraints estimated to have impacted revenue by $6 billion. The company achieved significant growth across all product categories, with notable performance in iPhone, Mac, and services, which reached an all-time high of $18.3 billion. The strong demand for new products, including the iPhone 13 lineup and MacBook Pro, contributed to the positive results.
features.management_outlook: Looking ahead, Apple anticipates that supply constraints will worsen in the December quarter, potentially exceeding the $6 billion impact experienced in Q4. Despite this, the company expects solid year-over-year revenue growth across most product categories, although a decline is anticipated for iPad due to ongoing supply issues. Management remains optimistic about customer demand and the performance of new product launches.
features.risks: Apple faces significant risks from ongoing supply chain disruptions, particularly related to semiconductor shortages and COVID-related manufacturing challenges. These issues could lead to further revenue impacts and affect the availability of products during the critical holiday season. Additionally, the company must navigate regulatory pressures regarding its App Store practices and privacy policies, which could influence its services revenue.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of Apple's performance include robust demand for its latest products, particularly the iPhone 13 and MacBook Pro, as well as strong growth in its services segment. The installed base of active devices reached an all-time high, contributing to increased customer engagement and satisfaction. Apple's continued investment in innovation and expansion into emerging markets also supports its growth trajectory.
